summary: The build failed during C++ compilation: clang++ reports an invalid explicit instantiation of 'operator()' in simd/moments files, causing ninja to stop and metadata generation to fail.
root_cause: C++ compilation failed with clang++ error: "explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member" in ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp (and referenced from moments_inst_neon.cpp and moments.cpp), causing the build to stop.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255:50: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Compiler error pointing to moments_simd.hpp line 255 showing the explicit instantiation is invalid.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14:43: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Same compiler error reported in moments_inst_neon.cpp, indicating the instantiation site.
Shows the object build failed for the neon SIMD moments compilation unit.
Build system stopped due to the compilation error.
The packaging/installation step failed because the build (a subprocess) exited with an error.

suggested_fix: Inspect and fix the explicit instantiation at ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp (around line 255) so it refers to a valid function/template/member (or remove/adjust the instantiation). Rebuild to verify the clang++ C++20 compilation succeeds; if this is a compiler compatibility issue, adjust the code or compiler flags to be compatible with clang++ on macOS.
